LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BW/IP INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DECEDENT IDENTIFIED DEFENDANT'S PUMPS AND THAT GASKETS AND PACKING MATERIALS WERE DISTURBED IN HIS PRESENCE. DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORY RESPONSES REFLECT SALES BY DEFENDANT OF PUMPS WITH ASBESTOS GASKETS/PACKING IN BOTH THE ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT AND AFTER MARKET. DEFENDANT FAILED TO CLARIFY WHETHER DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ORIGINAL AND/OR AFTER MARKET PRODUCTS. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET THE STANDARD SET FORTH IN SCHEIDING V DINWIDDIE CONSTRUCTION CO. (1999) 69 CAL. APP. 4TH 64, 80. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO FRAUD AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NOT POINTED TO SPECIFIC DISCOVERY RESPONSES THAT DIRECTLY ADDRESS THE ISSUES. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO PARAGRAPH 13 OF THACKER, DENIES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO GERCHOW DECLARATION EXHIBIT A, PAGE 244;21 THRU 245:5 AND DOES NOT RULE ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO AYE DECLARATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Off Calendar Aug-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Todd Thacker, Esq. of Jackson & Wallace, 415-982-6300 for defendant and moving party BW/IP International, Inc.
Paul Vaillancourt, Esq. of Brayton Pucell, 415-898-1555 for plaintiff and opposing party Carl Ransdell

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
DEFENDANT BW/IP INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DECEDENT IDENTIFIED DEFENDANT'S PUMPS AND THAT GASKETS AND PACKING MATERIALS WERE DISTURBED IN HIS PRESENCE. DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORY RESPONSES REFLECT SALES BY DEFENDANT OF PUMPS WITH ASBESTOS GASKETS/PACKING IN BOTH THE ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT AND AFTER MARKET. DEFENDANT FAILED TO CLARIFY WHETHER DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ORIGINAL AND/OR AFTER MARKET PRODUCTS. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET THE STANDARD SET FORTH IN SCHEIDING V DINWIDDIE CONSTRUCTION CO. (1999) 69 CAL. APP. 4TH 64, 80. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO FRAUD AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NOT POINTED TO SPECIFIC DISCOVERY RESPONSES THAT DIRECTLY ADDRESS THE ISSUES. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO PARAGRAPH 13 OF THACKER, DENIES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO GERCHOW DECLARATION EXHIBIT A, PAGE 244;21 THRU 245:5 AND DOES NOT RULE ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO AYE DECLARATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/PB)
